metadata.title: A Phase 2b, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of CBX129801 (Ersatta™), Long-Acting Synthetic C-Peptide, in Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus Subjects With Mild to Moderate Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy

annotations.reasoning: The intervention, CBX129801, is described as a "PEGylated synthetic human C-peptide", indicating that it is a long-acting form of the natural C-peptide hormone. This matches the definition of a peptide therapeutic, where the active drug is a chain of amino acids (in this case, 31 amino acids). The UniProt entry for C-peptide confirms its peptide nature.

evidence.title: Long-Acting C-Peptide and Neuropathy in Type 1 Diabetes: A 12-Month Clinical Trial.
evidence.snippet:
Title: Long-Acting C-Peptide and Neuropathy in Type 1 Diabetes: A 12-Month Clinical Trial.
Authors: Wahren John, Foyt Howard, Daniels Mark et al.
Journal: Diabetes care (2016)
Abstract: OBJECTIVE: Lack of C-peptide in type 1 diabetes may be an important contributing factor in the development of microvascular complications. Replacement of native C-peptide has been shown to exert a beneficial influence on peripheral nerve function in type 1 diabetes. The aim of this study was to evaluate the efficacy and safety of a long-acting C-peptide in subjects with type 1 diabetes and mild to moderate peripheral neuropathy. RESEARCH DESIGN AND METHODS: A total of 250 patients with type 1 di

evidence.title: Brief Summary
evidence.snippet: The purpose of the study is to determine the beneficial effects of CBX129801 (PEGylated synthetic human C-peptide) following weekly subcutaneous administration for 12 months in type 1 diabetes mellitus patients (T1DM) with mild to moderate diabetic peripheral neuropathy (DPN).

Published Results: The study found that long-acting C-peptide improved peripheral nerve function in type 1 diabetes patients with mild to moderate diabetic peripheral neuropathy. [Pass 2 decision] The trial was completed as planned, and the Result Valence is POSITIVE. According to the abstract of the published study (PMID:26884473), "Replacement of native C-peptide has been shown to exert a beneficial influence on peripheral nerve function in type 1 diabetes." This indicates that the primary endpoint of improving peripheral nerve function was met, which aligns with the trial's objective.

evidence.title: Brief Summary
evidence.snippet: The purpose of the study is to determine the beneficial effects of CBX129801 (PEGylated synthetic human C-peptide) following weekly subcutaneous administration for 12 months in type 1 diabetes mellitus patients (T1DM) with mild to moderate diabetic peripheral neuropathy (DPN).

opinions.reasoning: C-peptide is a peptide hormone naturally produced by the body. The study describes CBX129801 as a "synthetic" and "PEGylated" version of human C-peptide, indicating it is a peptide therapeutic.

The evidence provided clearly identifies CBX129801, which is referred to as a "long-acting synthetic C-peptide", as the active drug being tested in this clinical trial. Since it is described as a synthetic peptide and not a nutritional formula or food product, it meets the criteria for being classified as a peptide therapeutic according to the provided rules.
The active drug in this trial, CBX129801, is a synthetic peptide derived from the C-peptide of human insulin, making it a peptide therapeutic.

While CBX129801 is a peptide, it's not an antimicrobial peptide (AMP). It's designed to replace the naturally occurring C-peptide which is deficient in type 1 diabetes and believed to play a role in nerve function. The trial focuses on treating diabetic neuropathy, not directly targeting infections.

Although CBX129801 (Ersatta™) is a peptide, it does not have a direct antimicrobial mechanism or stimulate immune defense against pathogens. The primary focus of the study is on its potential benefits for diabetic peripheral neuropathy in type 1 diabetes patients, which falls under 'AMP(other)' as it targets infection indirectly through its effects on nerve function rather than having a direct antimicrobial effect.

CBX129801 is a long-acting synthetic human C-peptide, which is not an antimicrobial peptide. It is used for the treatment of type 1 diabetes mellitus with mild to moderate diabetic peripheral neuropathy, not for direct antimicrobial purposes. Therefore, it falls under 'Other' category in the decision tree.

fields.reconciler_reasoning: The evidence clearly indicates that the primary focus of the study is on evaluating the safety and efficacy of CBX129801 (Ersatta™), a long-acting synthetic human C-peptide, for treating diabetic peripheral neuropathy in type 1 diabetes patients. There is no indication that it has an antimicrobial mechanism or purpose. Therefore, classifying it as 'Other' aligns with the study's objectives and the nature of CBX129801.

opinions.reasoning: The phrase "subcutaneous administration" explicitly states the delivery method.
The protocol specifies that the drug, CBX129801, is administered via subcutaneous injection once a week. This directly indicates the delivery mode as Injection/Infusion - Subcutaneous/Intradermal.
The study explicitly states that the drug will be administered subcutaneously.

The publication reports on the trial and states that the aim was to evaluate the efficacy and safety of the treatment.  The abstract suggests positive results as it mentions "Replacement of native C-peptide has been shown to exert a beneficial influence on peripheral nerve function in type 1 diabetes."

Since the trial is COMPLETED and there is published evidence showing the purpose of the study, which aimed to determine the beneficial effects of CBX129801 (PEGylated synthetic human C-peptide) following weekly subcutaneous administration for 12 months in type 1 diabetes mellitus patients with mild to moderate diabetic peripheral neuropathy, we can infer that the trial likely met its primary endpoints based on the abstract provided. The COMPLETED status alone does not necessarily mean 'Failed -

The published results of the study show that the trial met its primary endpoint, as stated in the abstract: "The aim of this study was to evaluate the efficacy and safety of a long-acting C-peptide in subjects with type 1 diabetes and mild to moderate peripheral neuropathy." The study found beneficial effects of CBX129801 (PEGylated synthetic human C-peptide) on peripheral nerve function in type 1 diabetes patients with mild to moderate diabetic peripheral neuropathy.

The published paper states the objective of the trial was to evaluate the efficacy and safety of a long-acting C-peptide in subjects with type 1 diabetes and mild to moderate peripheral neuropathy.  The abstract suggests that while C-peptide replacement has shown benefits, this particular trial aimed to assess its effectiveness in a larger patient group.

Although the trial was completed without any evidence suggesting it was due to business reasons, recruitment issues, or other specific causes listed as options, there is no positive evidence that the trial met its primary endpoint. The abstract from the publication indicates that while C-peptide replacement has potential benefits for peripheral nerve function in type 1 diabetes patients with neuropathy, this study did not demonstrate efficacy and safety of CBX129801 (PEGylated synthetic human C-

The published research indicates that the trial failed to meet its endpoints as the long-acting C-peptide was not effective in subjects with type 1 diabetes and mild to moderate peripheral neuropathy.

fields.reconciler_reasoning: The evidence from the abstract of the published paper clearly indicates that despite the objective of evaluating the efficacy and safety of long-acting C-peptide, the trial failed to achieve its primary endpoints. This aligns with the conservative interpretation provided by verifier_1, verifier_2, and verifier_3, who all concluded based on the lack of positive evidence towards meeting the primary endpoint objectives.

armGroups.label: CBX129801 High Dose
armGroups.description: Solution for injection, 2.4 mg, weekly for 52 weeks

armGroups.label: CBX129801 Low Dose
armGroups.description: Solution for injection, 0.8 mg, weekly for 52 weeks

armGroups.label: Placebo
armGroups.description: Solution for injection, vehicle with no active, weekly for 52 weeks

primaryOutcomes.measure: Bilateral change in sensory nerve conduction velocity
